title: Cooking With Heat
editorialSummary: Cooking techniques grouped by the primary heat-transfer mechanism — conduction, convection, radiation, or induction; the false trail is that baking and roasting both use hot oven air (convection) but solvers may assume roasting is dry-radiant heat, when in fact both are convection-dominant — the true separation is between steaming (convection via steam) and grilling (radiation from direct flame).

meta.gptRationale: The cooking methods are mostly familiar, but distinguishing heat-transfer modes from liquid-medium techniques creates some conceptual friction.
